skill.name: epub-chapter-extractor
skill.description: Extract chapters from an EPUB file and save each chapter as a separate markdown document.
skill.summary: Split EPUB books into numbered markdown chapter files.
skill.author: Abilityai

security_audit.summary: Static filesystem findings are true positives because the script creates an output directory and writes markdown files. Static external command findings are expected for this skill because SKILL.md instructs the assistant to run uv and open the output folder. The hidden home directory and weak cryptography alerts are false positives, with no evidence of prompt injection, network access, credential access, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Writes to User-Selected Output Directory
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script creates an output directory and writes one markdown file per chapter. This is the intended behavior, but it can create or overwrite files if the assistant or user selects an unsafe output path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly calls os.makedirs and open(..., 'w') using an output path derived from user input. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but the write capability is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Assistant Is Instructed to Run Local Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md instructs the assistant to run uv with third-party Python packages and then open the output folder. The commands are relevant to the skill, but they execute code and depend on locally resolved packages.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in documented usage examples and execute the bundled Python script. I did not find shell metacharacter construction or hidden network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Hidden Home Directory Alert Is an Installation Path
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The ~/.claude path appears only as the expected installed skill directory in command examples. I did not find evidence of hidden file harvesting or unauthorized home directory traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both references are cd commands into the documented skill installation path. No code reads secrets or enumerates hidden files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Has No Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged line is the skill description and contains no cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, encryption logic, or security-sensitive comparison.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the flagged line and the scanned files found no weak cryptographic function. The static match appears unrelated to actual code behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Several static external-command hits are markdown fences, inline path examples, filename examples, or output format examples. These lines are documentation text and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown syntax or sample output names, not executable code paths. The only real command examples are separately evaluated as medium risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The script creates directories and writes markdown files to a path derived from the input EPUB or optional output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem write operations are explicit and central to the skill. The risk depends on the chosen output directory.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells the assistant to run uv and the bundled Python script, which is necessary for extraction but should be visible to users before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and limited to the extraction workflow. No evidence found of obfuscated or unrelated command execution.

content.value_statement: Large EPUB files are difficult to review, quote, or reorganize as a single document. This skill splits an EPUB into numbered markdown chapter files for easier editing and analysis.
actual_capabilities: Reads an EPUB file with ebooklib.
Uses the EPUB table of contents to identify chapters.
Converts chapter HTML into markdown text.
Writes each chapter to a numbered markdown file.
Creates a default output folder beside the source EPUB.
Reports extraction progress and failed chapters in the terminal.
limitations: Requires uv and Python package downloads or cached packages.
Depends on a usable EPUB table of contents.
Removes images, scripts, styles, navigation, and embedded media.
May not preserve complex EPUB layouts or nested section boundaries perfectly.

use_cases.title: Prepare Manuscript Review Files
use_cases.description: Split a book into chapter files for comments, summaries, or editorial review.

use_cases.title: Build Research Notes from Ebooks
use_cases.description: Convert chapters into markdown files that can be searched, annotated, and linked in a notes system.

use_cases.title: Create Publishing Workflow Inputs
use_cases.description: Extract clean chapter text before restructuring content for documentation or publishing tools.

prompt_templates.title: Extract One EPUB
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner extraction with the default output folder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract chapters from /path/to/book.epub into separate markdown files.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Output Folder
prompt_templates.scenario: Specify where generated chapter files should be saved.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract chapters from /path/to/book.epub and save them in /path/to/output-folder.

prompt_templates.title: Review Extraction Results
prompt_templates.scenario: Run extraction and summarize any failed chapters.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract chapters from /path/to/book.epub, then tell me how many chapter files were created and list any failures.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Markdown for Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract chapters from /path/to/book.epub into /path/to/draft-chapters, then inspect the output filenames for ordering problems.

output_examples.input: Extract chapters from a novel EPUB.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a folder named after the book and saves numbered markdown files such as introduction and chapter one.

output_examples.input: Extract chapters into a chosen project folder.
output_examples.output: The skill writes chapter files to the selected folder and reports the number of successful and failed extractions.

output_examples.input: Process an EPUB without a usable table of contents.
output_examples.output: The skill stops with an error explaining that the EPUB has no table of contents.

best_practices: Use a dedicated empty output folder to avoid overwriting existing markdown files.
Confirm the EPUB path and output directory before running the command.
Review extracted chapters for formatting issues before using them in production.
anti_patterns: Do not run extraction against files you do not have permission to process.
Do not choose a broad system folder as the output directory.
Do not assume images or complex layouts will be preserved.

faq.question: Does this skill modify the original EPUB file?
faq.answer: No. It reads the EPUB and writes separate markdown files.

faq.question: Where are the output files saved?
faq.answer: By default, it creates a folder named after the EPUB beside the source file.

faq.question: Can I choose a different output folder?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide an output directory after the EPUB path.

faq.question: Does it keep images from the EPUB?
faq.answer: No. The script removes images and embedded media during cleanup.

faq.question: What tools does it require?
faq.answer: It uses uv to run Python with ebooklib, BeautifulSoup, html2text, and lxml.

faq.question: Is this safe for private ebooks?
faq.answer: The script runs locally and has no network code, but it writes files to the chosen output folder.
